data.description:
Follow-up phases of the c11mux Tier 1 persistence plan (docs/c11mux-tier1-persistence-plan.md).

Phase 1 (stable panel UUIDs via constructor refactor) ships in PR #10. This ticket tracks the remaining work:

- Phase 2: Persist SurfaceMetadataStore (lossless — full value-type fidelity + source attribution). Makes M1/M3/M7 features durable across restart.
- Phase 3: Persist statusEntries (with fidelity fix for url/priority/format; stamp restored entries with staleFromRestart).
- Phase 4 (Tier 1b, re-justify): Claude session index (observe from ~/.claude/projects; flagged off; privacy posture + opt-out).
- Phase 5 (Tier 1b): Recovery UI — copy-first 'Resume Claude session' chip in the title bar; CLI 'cmux surface recreate'.

Each phase lands as its own PR. Tier 1a (Phases 1-3) ships first; Tier 1b (Phases 4-5) re-justifies after 1a lands with measurement data.

Blocked on Phase 1 (PR #10) merging. Unblock once Phase 1 is on main and stable.
data.title: Tier 1 persistence: Phases 2-5 (metadata store, status entries, Claude session index, recovery UI)

data.reason: Depends on Tier 1 Phase 1 (PR https://github.com/Stage-11-Agentics/c11mux/pull/10) merging before Phase 2 can start. Unblock when Phase 1 lands on main.

provenance.reason: Depends on Tier 1 Phase 1 (PR https://github.com/Stage-11-Agentics/c11mux/pull/10) merging before Phase 2 can start. Unblock when Phase 1 lands on main.

data.to:
Follow-up phases of the c11mux Tier 1 persistence plan (`docs/c11mux-tier1-persistence-plan.md`). Phase 1 (stable panel UUIDs) shipped in PR #10 and unblocks all of this work.

## Why this exists

Today the M-series features don't survive restart:
- M1 `terminal_type`, M3 sidebar chips (`model`, `role`, `status`), and M7 title/description all route through `SurfaceMetadataStore`, which is in-memory only. Every agent- or operator-written customization dies on reboot.
- Even without resuming live PTYs, if c11mux knew *what* was running in each surface (cwd, last command, Claude session id), it could offer a one-tap recreate/resume — turning catastrophic loss into a minor speed bump.

Phase 1 gave us stable panel UUIDs so external consumers can cache them across restarts. Phases 2-5 build the durability and recovery layers on top.

## Scope

### Tier 1a — Durability (ships first, bounded re-justification at end)

**Phase 2 — Persist `SurfaceMetadataStore` (lossless)**
Done when: canonical + custom metadata keys and their `{source, ts}` sidecars round-trip through disk. `tests_v2/test_m2_metadata_persistence.py` passes against a tagged DEBUG build.
- Add `PersistedJSONValue` + `PersistedMetadataSource` Codable types (plan §Phase 2).
- Add `metadata` / `metadataSources` optional fields to `SessionPanelSnapshot`.
- Add `metadataStoreRevision: UInt64` (no-op write dedup) and plumb into `AppDelegate.sessionFingerprint`.
- Ship OSLog metric for total metadata bytes per autosave.
- Amend M2 spec (`docs/c11mux-module-2-metadata-spec-amendment-tier1.md`) — the current line "in-memory only" is superseded.

**Phase 3 — Persist `statusEntries` (with fidelity fix)**
Done when: status pills restored on launch, flagged `staleFromRestart`, cleared by the first real write even when payload matches existing. `url` / `priority` / `format` fields round-trip.
- Extend `SessionStatusEntrySnapshot` with `url`, `priority`, `format`, `staleFromRestart`.
- Replace `statusEntries.removeAll()` in `Workspace.restoreSessionSnapshot` with stamping restore.
- Patch `shouldReplaceStatusEntry` so `staleFromRestart: true → false` always wins.
- Sidebar renders stale entries with reduced emphasis (opacity/italic).

**Depends on:** Phase 2 ships first for joint schema coordination on `SessionPanelSnapshot`. Companion workspace-metadata plan touches the same struct — whichever lands first does the extension.

### Tier 1b — Recovery UX (re-justify at the Tier 1a → 1b boundary)

Tier 1b prerequisites: Tier 1a shipped and stable ≥1 release; M7 merged (done); cwd-slug algorithm confirmed (done 2026-04-18); measurement data from Tier 1a in hand.

**Phase 4 — Claude session index (observe-from-outside)**
Done when: focusing a terminal in a cwd with recent Claude transcripts stamps `agent.claude.session_id` via M2 precedence (source: `.heuristic`). `tests_v2/test_claude_session_association.py` passes.
- New `Sources/ClaudeSessionIndex.swift` with bounds per plan §Phase 4 (depth-1, 30s cache, 250ms timeout, mandatory first-line `cwd` verification, no symlinks, 10 MiB file cap, read-only).
- Namespaced canonical keys: `agent.claude.session_id`, `agent.claude.session_started_at`.
- Feature flag `CMUX_EXPERIMENTAL_SESSION_INDEX=1` (off by default); opt-out `CMUX_DISABLE_SESSION_INDEX=1`.
- Privacy doc `docs/c11mux-privacy.md`.
- Remote-daemon workspaces explicitly unsupported in v1 — local Mac scan only.

**Phase 5 — Recovery UI with shell-prompt safety gate**
Done when: restored surface with `agent.claude.session_id` offers a compact resume chip in the title bar. Default action copies `claude --resume <id>` to clipboard; "Send to pane" requires confirmation. Fallback chain: resume → reopen-in-dir → restore-cwd → disabled. `cmux surface recreate` CLI mirrors the output.
- All user-facing strings localized (English + Japanese).
- Shares `CMUX_EXPERIMENTAL_SESSION_INDEX` flag with Phase 4.

**Depends on:** Phase 4 ships before Phase 5.

## Carry-over from Phase 1 (handback findings)

These are load-bearing for Phase 2 and must not be rediscovered:

1. **Workspace UUIDs are NOT stable across restart.** `TabManager.restoreSessionSnapshot` mints fresh `Workspace()` per snapshot (no `id:` injection). `SurfaceMetadataStore` keys are `(workspaceId, surfaceId)` — if workspace IDs regenerate every restart, the Phase 1 win is half-negated for workspace-scoped metadata. Phase 2 must either do the equivalent constructor refactor one level up (at `Workspace`), or the store needs a different keying strategy. **Decide this before writing the persistence layer.**

2. **`debug.session.round_trip` is in-memory only.** Phase 1 added this DEBUG-only socket command for the Python test. It's a faithful proxy for the contract but doesn't exercise disk serialization. Phase 2 should add `debug.session.save_and_load` (actual file round-trip) so metadata tests can catch JSON encode/decode bugs that the in-memory path misses.

3. **`CMUX_DISABLE_STABLE_PANEL_IDS` is Phase 1's rollback safety net.** One-release deletion followup — add as a separate small PR once Phase 1 has been stable on main for a release, or fold into Phase 2.

4. **Rollback env vars are app-scope, not CLI-scope.** Any new `CMUX_*` flag added by Phases 2-5 must be set in the app's environment at launch (`launchctl setenv` or parent shell), not on `cmux` CLI invocation. Document in operator-facing rollback notes.

## Ship order

Each phase lands as its own PR. Do not bundle. After Phase 3 ships, **stop** and re-justify Tier 1b using Tier 1a measurement data before starting Phase 4.

## Companion / out-of-scope

- Live PTY survival is Tier 2 (move PTY ownership into cmuxd). Not this ticket.
- `firstUserMessagePreview` from Claude transcripts is deferred — privacy gate.
- No `SessionIndex` protocol until a second agent integration (Codex) arrives.
- `lattice-stage-11-plugin` is out of date relative to c11mux and is not a blocker.

## Unblock condition

Phase 1 (PR https://github.com/Stage-11-Agentics/c11mux/pull/10) merged to main and stable. At that point, transition to `backlog` and scope Phase 2 specifically.

provenance.reason: Beef up description with motivation, per-phase acceptance criteria, intra-phase dependencies, and Phase 1 handback findings so the ticket is standalone-useful.

data.to: Follow-up phases of the c11mux Tier 1 persistence plan (`docs/c11mux-tier1-persistence-plan.md`). Phase 1 (PR #10), Phase 1.5 (PR #12), and Phase 2 (PR #13) have all shipped. This parent tracks what remains: Phase 3 completes Tier 1a durability, Phases 4-5 add Tier 1b recovery UX (re-justify at the 1a/1b boundary), and the cleanup removes the one-release rollback safety nets. See subtasks for scope per phase.
